qa.overall_assessment: All 8 acceptance criteria pass. Dead variable removed, central fast-path added correctly with push exclusion and no return-after-push, per-function calls retained, secondary bash calls removed, global short-circuit documented, IS_SUBAGENT moved to top of script, duplicate inline parses eliminated, subagent firewall intact, OBJ-8 compound-command path verified by code-path trace.

checks.evidence: Grep of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py for 'sid = _get_session_id' in _evaluate_command body (lines 657-674): zero matches. The 4 remaining hits (lines 131, 148, 460, 572) are all in _has_do_consent, _check_git_allowlist, _evaluate_commit, and _evaluate_push respectively — none in _evaluate_command. _evaluate_command dispatches to all 6 evaluate-* functions (lines 663-674) unchanged except M1+M2.

Line 661: 'if not _looks_like_git_push(command) and _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return' — first substantive check in _evaluate_command. Per-function _check_git_allowlist calls confirmed present at line 455 (_evaluate_commit), 480 (_evaluate_merge), 602 (_evaluate_reset_hard), 615 (_evaluate_direct_ref_mutation), 648 (_evaluate_forbidden_plumbing). _evaluate_push call at line 670 has NO return after it; lines 671-674 dispatch to _evaluate_merge and _evaluate_commit. Comment at lines 658-660 explains push exemption. Total _check_git_allowlist grep: 7 hits — definition at 141, dispatcher at 661, push-internal at 583, and the 5 per-function retentions.

In _evaluate_push (lines 571-598): _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 576, _check_git_allowlist at line 583. Ordering preserved: force/delete/ref-rewrite push blocked by line 576 before any allowlist check at line 583. The central fast-path in _evaluate_command at line 661 is conditioned on 'not _looks_like_git_push', so push commands never reach the central check — they fall through to _evaluate_push directly.

Grep for 'check_and_consume_allowlist' in pretool-bash-safety.sh: 6 hits total. Of these: line 139 is the function definition, line 392/434 are comments referencing the function, lines 592/593 are comments, line 618 is the single invocation ('check_and_consume_allowlist "$COMMAND" && exit 0'). The 6 former secondary call sites at ~805, ~984, ~995, ~1007, ~1027, ~1110 are absent — confirmed by reading the file sections covering all those former locations (lines 793-820, 975-1030). Comment at lines 613-617 documents the global short-circuit as the sole PreToolUse allowlist match/approval call site and explains that per-rule secondary calls were removed in task 20260518-094616.

Global short-circuit at line 618 is unconditional: it appears at line 618, after the /do bypass block ends at line 611, and before Layer 1.A daemon-restart prohibition at line 620. No conditional wraps it. A systemctl restart nginx command with a valid /allow grant would fire check_and_consume_allowlist at line 618 and exit 0 before reaching any block rule. Confirmed by reading the control flow at lines 599-640.

IS_SUBAGENT= assigned at line 20 of pretool-bash-safety.sh (within first 30 lines, after PYTHON_BIN resolution at lines 10-13). Grep for 'agent_id' in pretool-bash-safety.sh: single hit at line 20 (the IS_SUBAGENT= assignment). check_and_consume_allowlist function body (lines 139-368): contains 'if [ "$IS_SUBAGENT" = "1" ]; then return 1; fi' at line 145 — no inline agent_id parse. _DO_IS_SUB does NOT appear anywhere in the file (grep returns no matches). The /do bypass block at line 601 uses 'if [ "$IS_SUBAGENT" != "1" ]' — no inline parse.

check_and_consume_allowlist at line 145: 'if [ "$IS_SUBAGENT" = "1" ]; then return 1; fi'. IS_SUBAGENT is set at line 20 from '$PYTHON_BIN -c ... print("1" if d.get("agent_id") else "0")'. When agent_id is present in payload, IS_SUBAGENT="1", and the function returns 1 (non-match, no bypass granted).

Code-path trace for 'git push origin main; git commit -m "x"' with CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 + push grant, no /allow grant, no commit grant: (1) Line 661: not _looks_like_git_push(command)=False (command contains 'git push') → central fast-path skipped. (2) Lines 663-668: _looks_like_git_forbidden_plumbing returns False ('git commit -m' does not match commit-tree/cherry-pick/rebase/pull/filter-branch/filter-repo/replace/fast-import/revert/am); reset/ref checks also False. (3) Line 669-670: _looks_like_git_push=True → _evaluate_push called → CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 + valid push grant → all validations pass → push succeeds, _evaluate_push returns normally. (4) NO return after line 670 → execution falls through. (5) Line 671: _looks_like_git_merge=False. (6) Line 673: _looks_like_git_commit=True → _evaluate_commit called. (7) In _evaluate_commit: msg extracted; blessed-bridge regex does not match; _check_git_allowlist returns False (no /allow grant); _find_grant('commit', sid) returns None (no commit grant); _find_grant_any('commit') returns None; _block_default_deny_commit fires → exit 2 with 'BLOCKED: agent git commit'. OBJ-8 regression eliminated.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex CF-1: _check_git_allowlist() uses unbounded re.search() (line 165-168) with no SIGALRM timeout. The new central fast-path at line 661 calls this for all non-push commands when a grant file exists, slightly widening the surface compared to the pre-refactor per-function calls. The unbounded regex risk is pre-existing (all 5 prior per-function call sites had the same issue). Codex demonstrated a ReDoS grant pattern can hang the hook on non-git commands. Fix: add SIGALRM timeout to _check_git_allowlist's re.search call, mirroring pretool-bash-safety.sh's V1 timeout design.

root_cause_verification.rationale: All 4 architectural issues in scope (allow-1 dead variable, allow-2 unstructured enforcement, allow-3 secondary dead calls, allow-5 duplicate IS_SUBAGENT parse) are addressed. allow-4 required no code change. allow-6 is Won't Have per BA spec.

build_verification.skip_reason: Non-UI hook files only. BA spec setup section: 'non-UI — hook; cycle does not produce rendered UI changes, browser interaction, Playwright invocation, screenshot evidence, or any change to user-triggered code paths in the web/pipeline sense.' Dev report confirms python_syntax: ok, bash_syntax: ok.

Dev report self-verification: bash_syntax: ok. File reads and executes correctly per dev report.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: CF-1 (major): _check_git_allowlist uses unbounded re.search; the new central fast-path widens this to all non-push commands when a grant file exists. Codex demonstrated a ReDoS pattern can hang the hook on non-git commands. Proposed fix: add SIGALRM timeout to _check_git_allowlist.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: CF-1 classified as out_of_scope: the unbounded re.search is pre-existing (present in all 5 prior call sites before this refactor); this cycle's scope is architectural cleanup only (no new security controls). Routed to out_of_scope_observations with security_relevant:true for future cycle. Verdict unchanged: PASS.
